Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.transformer.tests

Class SimpleSequentTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.transformer.tests.AbstractTransformerTests
org.eventb.core.seqprover.transformer.tests.SimpleSequentTests
public class SimpleSequentTests extends AbstractTransformerTests
Unit tests for interface ISimpleSequent.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    discardTranform()
    Ensures that the discard transformer produces an empty sequent.
    void
    emptySequent()
    Ensures that empty simple sequents can be created.
    void
    fixedTranform()
    Ensures that the fixed transformer produces the expected sequent.
    void
    goalOnly()
    Ensures that sequents reduced to a goal can be created.
    void
    identityTranform()
    Ensures that the identity transformer produces the same sequent.
    void
    notEquals()
    Ensures that method equals() behaves properly.
    void
    regular()
    Ensures that regular sequents can be created.
    void
    string()
    Ensures that toString() produces expected strings.
    void
    trivial()
    Ensures that trivial sequents can be created.
    void
    typeEnvironment()
    Ensures that type environments are correctly inferred.

  • Method Details

    • emptySequent

      public void emptySequent()
      Ensures that empty simple sequents can be created.

    • goalOnly

      public void goalOnly()
      Ensures that sequents reduced to a goal can be created.

    • trivial

      public void trivial()
      Ensures that trivial sequents can be created.

    • regular

      public void regular()
      Ensures that regular sequents can be created.

    • typeEnvironment

      public void typeEnvironment()
      Ensures that type environments are correctly inferred.

    • identityTranform

      public void identityTranform()
      Ensures that the identity transformer produces the same sequent.

    • fixedTranform

      public void fixedTranform()
      Ensures that the fixed transformer produces the expected sequent.

    • discardTranform

      public void discardTranform()
      Ensures that the discard transformer produces an empty sequent.

    • string

      public void string()
      Ensures that toString() produces expected strings.

    • notEquals

      public void notEquals()
      Ensures that method equals() behaves properly. Positive cases are already handled by the other tests, here we just check negative cases.
